action.content: Write a funny song-style poem for kids about why you shouldn't eat a lot of sweets. The poem should have four sections, with each section marked with SECTION X.
action.label: context

action.content:
SECTION 1
Oh, the candy store is bright and grand,
With chocolate bars and gum in hand!
But if you eat a whole big pile,
Your tummy starts to feel a while...
Like a bouncy ball inside your chest,
That makes you feel a little best...
No, a little *worst*! It starts to rumble,
Like a dragon trying to come *gumble*!

SECTION 2
Then comes the sugar crash, so fast,
You feel like you might fall and last...
No, you feel like you might fall asleep,
While everyone else is wide awake and deep!
Your eyes get heavy, your head goes round,
You can't even touch the ground!
You try to run, but you just sway,
Like a jellybean that lost its way!

SECTION 3
And then the teeth begin to ache,
With a tiny, ticklish, sugary shake!
The sugar bugs are having a party,
Making holes in your happy factory!
They eat the enamel, one by one,
Until your smile is looking done!
So brush them clean before they grow,
Or you'll have to say "Ouch!" to your jaw!

SECTION 4
So save the sweets for just a treat,
Not every single day you eat!
Have one small piece, then wash it down,
With water, not a sugary frown!
Your tummy will be happy and light,
And you'll run fast all through the night!
So say goodbye to the candy mountain,
And keep your body feeling clean and saint-like!
action.label: response
